steps.title: Identify the grammar issue
steps.content: The blank fills the subject + linking verb slot before "68th in a global ranking." The subject is "the constitution of Lithuania" (singular), so the blank needs a singular subject pronoun plus a linking verb.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A: their — possessive pronoun, plural. No linking verb, so the sentence has no main verb. Eliminated.

B: its — possessive pronoun, singular. Still no linking verb; "its 68th in a global ranking" is not a complete clause. Eliminated.

C: it's — contraction of "it is." Plugging in: "it is 68th in a global ranking" — complete clause, singular subject + linking verb, grammatically correct. Correct.

D: they're — contraction of "they are," plural. The constitution is one document, so plural disagrees with the antecedent. Eliminated.
